# sysctl settings are defined through files in
# /usr/lib/sysctl.d/, /run/sysctl.d/, and /etc/sysctl.d/.
#
# Vendors settings live in /usr/lib/sysctl.d/.

scan_output: Vulnerability sysctl_net_ipv4_conf_all_forwarding not found in single-rule scan (possibly fixed)

llm_verdict.message:
## REMEDIATION COMPLETE ✅

The OpenSCAP finding `sysctl_net_ipv4_conf_all_forwarding` has been successfully remediated:

**What was fixed:**
- ✅ Runtime setting: `net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding = 0` (applied immediately)
- ✅ Persistent configuration: Verified `/etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-ipv4-forwarding.conf` exists with correct setting
- ✅ Existing configs: Confirmed `/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf` and `/etc/sysctl.conf` already contain `net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding = 0`

**Verification:**
- Current runtime status: `net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding = 0` (confirmed)
- No duplicate or conflicting entries found

**Security Impact:**
This hardening measure prevents the system from acting as an IPv4 router, reducing the attack surface by disabling packet forwarding capabilities.

The system is now properly secured against this vulnerability and will maintain the setting across reboots.
